items.claim: Conor McGregor effectively neutralizes conventional fighters' right hands by using kicks and body strikes to counter them.
items.quote: Every single thing he does is to take away the right hand. How do you take away a right hand of a of a conventional fighter? You kick and you attack the side that they need.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is vulnerable when forced to move backward.
items.quote: If you can continually keep Connor moving back you have a chance.

items.claim: Dustin Poirier and Nate Diaz successfully neutralized Conor McGregor's counters by using a bladed stance.
items.quote: The way that they did that is because of their bladed stance in the opposite direction. It made Connor uncomfortable. He couldn't find the same openings.

items.claim: Max Holloway is at a disadvantage fighting Conor McGregor at 170 lbs because he has never competed at that weight class.
items.quote: We're seeing these fights similar now in Max Holloway, but at 170 lbs. We have never seen Max Holloway fight there.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is using performance-enhancing drugs to recover from a broken shin.
items.quote: Well, we've heard him admit it. I had to use juice. The doctors prescribed it to me to heal my bone.
